title: An efficient authentication and key-distribution protocol for wireless multimedia sensor network
author: Patil, Basavaraj
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: To provide security and privacy for multimedia data transmission, efficient techniques for authorizing and authenticating network users and nodes are required. These challenges have made it a vital and significant area of research in the present decade. Due to resource constraints, existing systems are unable to provide adequate protection against vulnerable behaviors and security assaults such as black-hole, Sybil, man-in-the-middle, and other similar attacks. In this paper, an effective enhanced engineered cementitious composites (ECC) and crypto-based authentication with a key exchange mechanism is proposed. The method boosts the effective authentication mechanism and reduces the number of vulnerable activities in the network. The simulation results demonstrate that the suggested technique is robust to malicious assaults and performs mutual authentication efficiently. A costbenefit analysis validates that the processing, communication, and storage requirements are much reduced when compared to existing approaches. Furthermore, an informal security analysis demonstrates that the suggested protocol is secure and adaptable to real-time scenarios.
